questions.question: When using the 'Replace' function (Ctrl + H) in MS PowerPoint, which of the following options allows users to replace only whole words and not parts of words?

When working with text wrapping around an image in MS Word, which 'Layout Option' provides the most flexibility in precisely positioning the image relative to the text and other elements, ignoring the text flow?
questions.option_2: Behind Text or In Front of Text

questions.question: If you move text using 'Cut and Paste', where does the text go before being pasted in MS Office?

When utilising the 'Paste Special' function in MS Word to insert content copied from a spreadsheet, which of the following paste options would preserve the original formula calculations while displaying the calculated values in the Word document?
When applying a watermark to an MS Word document, which of the following settings offers the LEAST control over its precise placement and size on each page?
questions.option_1: Inserting a graphic as a header or footer and positioning it behind the text
questions.option_2: Inserting a custom picture watermark and adjusting its scale and wash-out settings
questions.option_3: Inserting a custom text watermark with specific layout options
questions.option_4: Utilising a predefined watermark template from the 'Watermark' gallery

questions.question: When saving an MS PowerPoint presentation, which of the following options would be most suitable if the primary goal is to ensure the presentation opens in slideshow view by default for recipients who may not have MS PowerPoint installed?
When initiating a new presentation in MS PowerPoint, which of the following is the most accurate description of the 'Design Ideas' feature's primary function?
questions.option_1: To automatically translate the presentation content into multiple languages
questions.option_2: To suggest various pre-designed templates and layouts based on the content on the current slide
questions.option_3: To automatically populate slides with relevant content based on the presentation topic
questions.option_4: To provide real-time collaboration tools for multiple users working on the same presentation simultaneously

questions.question: When you reuse slides from another presentation in MS PowerPoint 365, which of the following options ensures the imported slides retain their original formatting?
questions.option_1: Click 'Keep Source Formatting' in the Reuse Slides pane
questions.option_3: Import slides as images to lock their look
questions.option_4: Apply the destination theme after inserting

questions.question: When saving an MS PowerPoint presentation, what is the primary purpose of the 'Save as Picture Presentation' option?
questions.option_1: To embed a static image of the first slide as a preview
questions.option_2: To save each slide as a separate image file within a folder
questions.option_3: To optimise the presentation for viewing on low-bandwidth internet connections
questions.option_4: To prevent any further editing of the presentation content

questions.question: How do you add text to a slide in MS PowerPoint?
questions.option_1: Click inside a text placeholder and start typing
questions.option_3: Drag an image onto the slide

questions.question: Which of the following elements, when poorly implemented, is most likely to undermine the credibility and impact of an otherwise well-researched MS PowerPoint presentation?
questions.option_1: Embedding multimedia elements such as videos
questions.option_2: The inclusion of speaker notes for reference
questions.option_3: Inconsistent use of slide masters and templates
questions.option_4: The choice of transition effects between slides

questions.question: Which of the following elements is NOT inherently part of a basic 'Blank Presentation' upon its initial creation in MS PowerPoint?
Which method CANNOT be used to create a new slide in MS PowerPoint 365?
questions.option_1: Clicking 'New Slide' on the Home tab
questions.option_3: Right-clicking in the slide sorter pane and selecting 'New Slide'

questions.question: Which file format is used for saving MS PowerPoint templates?
Which feature in MS-PowerPoint 2019 allows you to reuse slides from another presentation without copying content manually?
Which shortcut key combination is used to insert a hyperlink in MS PowerPoint 365?
If you want your audience to NOT see your notes in the MS PowerPoint 365 slides, which view should you use during slide show?
Which shortcut key is used to create a new blank presentation quickly in MS PowerPoint 365?
Which sequence of steps correctly initiates a blank presentation in MS PowerPoint 2016 or later?
Which keyboard shortcut is used to save an MS PowerPoint presentation quickly?
Which of the following methods is the quickest way to open an existing MS PowerPoint presentation?
questions.option_1: Double-click the .pptx file in File Explorer
questions.option_4: Open MS PowerPoint and click Blank Presentation

questions.question: Which keyboard shortcut is used in MS PowerPoint 365 to start editing text inside a selected shape or text box?
In MS PowerPoint, what is the significance of the aspect ratio setting (e.g. 16 : 9, 4 : 3) when you are setting up your slide size?
questions.option_1: It determines the file size of the saved presentation.
questions.option_2: It controls the number of slides that can be included in the presentation.
questions.option_3: It defines the proportions of the slide displayed on the screen, influencing how the content is laid out and how it appears on different display devices.
questions.option_4: It sets the default font size and style for all text boxes in the presentation.

questions.question: In Microsoft PowerPoint, which view is best suited for reorganising slides and getting an overview of the entire presentation structure?
In MS PowerPoint 2019, when you press the Enter key while typing in a text box, what happens?
questions.option_1: The cursor moves to the next line within the same text box.
questions.option_3: The text is entered into a new slide.
questions.option_4: The text box is automatically resized.
